{{Quote|It appears you're ready to begin discussions. Better I do not hear them, I think. If you'll excuse me, I have a space station to run.|Pol Temm|<ref name="Legacy 9" />}}
'''Pol Temm''' was the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] of [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]]<ref name="CSWE" /> as of [[137 ABY]]. He had spent most of his life on the station, working on a number of posts from a [[Casino/Legends|casino]] bouncer to become the leader of the station security. When his predecessor was assassinated by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] during the [[Sith–Imperial War]], he activated a long-developed plan to transfer the ownership of the station to himself. His policy onboard the Wheel was that it was to remain neutral, and no weapons or fighting were allowed or he would eject the violator into [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="Legacy Era CG" />

Temm monitored the meeting between [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]] of the [[Core Fleet|Galactic Alliance Core Fleet]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Mingo Bovark]] of [[Roan Fel]]'s [[Empire-in-exile|faction]] of the [[Fel Empire|new Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Legacy 9" />

After the meeting was [[Skirmish on The Wheel|sabotaged]] by agents [[Morrigan Corde]] and [[Jor Torlin]] by making it appear that the Imperial ship fired on the Galactic Alliance ship, Temm ordered that all ships of Fel's empire be banned from the station.<ref name="Legacy 10">[[Legacy (2006) 10|''Legacy'' (2006) 10]]</ref>

By [[138 ABY]], Pol Temm had disappeared after he tried to use an aged<ref name="Legacy War 3">[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]]</ref> [[Nebula-class Star Destroyer|''Defender''-class Star Destroyer]]<ref name="Vegas">{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-1|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> to enforce his "no weapons" policy<ref name="Legacy War 3" /><ref name="Vegas" /> against a group of [[Mandalorian Supremacists]]. He was succeeded by [[Attatag Gosem]], who did not enforce Temm's "no weapons" rule within the The Wheel.<ref name="Vegas" />
